notes:
Standard starter.
Any combo listed with this starter can be directly swapped to use any other starter listed below.

Slightly more corner carry. Less damage for combos that use resources.
When close to the opponent, walk forwards instead of dash to sacrifice some corner carry to build drive gauge.

Building Windclad mid-combo without spending drive gauge.
The opponent must be very close to the corner, so this can't punish forward moving reversals like Ken's OD Dragon Punch.

Close-range or corner starter that deals 100 more damage but builds less drive gauge.
Can be used in place of 6HP in any combo if close enough or near the corner.
4HP launches slightly higher than 6HP, making it required for some combos.

Long-range whiff punish starter using a non-canceled Drive Rush.
Loses some damage due to DR scaling.
2HP launches even higher than 4HP and is required by some combos.
For maximum launch height 2HP must connect with its first active frame, hence the delay.

Full-screen whiff punish starter done by canceling into Drive Rush instead (e.g. Dhalsim limbs).
Doing this close enough keeps the opponent grounded for a Heavy Starter Combo, which is required for SA3.

Point blank combo that builds Windclad, sometimes leading to a more optimal combo.
Can also be used to build 2 Windclad in one combo with a 214HP/PP followup.
5MK and 2MK are more consistent midscreen due to the special cancel moving towards the opponent.

Standard Drive Impact starter. A delay is sometimes needed to avoid launching the opponent too high.
Other air normals can also be used to adjust the launch height.
j.2HP provides more juggle time due to hitting 1 frame later, but it has less horizontal range so doesn't always reach.
j.MK sacrifices damage to consistently set up a launch slightly higher than 4HP.

Alex, Dee Jay, JP, Marisa, Sagat and Zangief are taller so get hit 1 frame early, resulting in a lower launch height from 6HP. This is good in some cases as it sets up the correct height for certain combos without requiring a delay. In other cases make sure to use j.2HP, which provides the exact same height as j.HK does against other characters.
Whether to dash depends on the distance the opponent travels to the wall.
Works when the opponent travels a long distance to the wall.
Technically not a crumple, but leads into the same combos.
Also technically not a crumple, but leads into the same combos.

route: (PC 5HP , 6HP ) >> W. 623PP > W. j.PPP > W. j.PP/ PPP

PC 5HP , dl. 4HP > W. 623HP , W. 623PP > *. j.PPP , SA1 / SA2
PC 5HP > 214[PP] , 5HP > (dl.) W. 623HP / MP , W. 623PP > j.PPP , SA1 / SA2

notes: Less damage than DR~4HP but conserves some drive gauge.

Less damage again, but can burn the opponent out more easily, especially when combined with a SA combo ender.
The typical follow-up route is j.HK, 6HP into combo ender.

Keeps the opponent grounded for SA3 and gives them less time to recover drive gauge during the combo.
Does work with 2HP but the longer animation lets the opponent recover more drive.
4HP > SA2/SA3 does not allow the opponent to recover any drive gauge at all.
4HP > SA1 lets them recover 1 pixel of drive, but this can be avoided with 5HP > SA1.

For burning out the opponent without using SA.
The opponent regains a tiny amount of drive during 4HP. If it looks like they might recover enough to avoid burnout then skip 4HP and go directly into a second DI.

Optimal damage for SA3 combos.
Can punish 2 frames faster if the opponent's attack is parried.

Situational starter if the opponent whiffs something during the charge.
After a blocked max range 2HP this can dodge and punish some Drive Reversals.
A faster option that deals a bit less damage than 4HP.

Sometimes the simplest option is the best one.
Slower and less damaging at long range but has more reach than 5HP.

Optimal damage for corner combos into SA1 or SA2.
Otherwise useful for punishing sweeps or conserving drive gauge.
Less damage than 5MK but more reach.
More damage than the above at point blank range.
Can stay close enough to chain 2 lights, which also sets up late-hit Windclad 623P combos.

route: PC 2MP > SA1 PC 2MP > *. SA2 PC 2MP > SA3
notes: SA2 requires Windclad to combo from a reasonable range.
Can stay close enough to chain 2 lights, which also sets up late-hit Windclad 623P combos.

Outdamages 360PP when resources are available.
When punishing a Drive Reversal it usually sets up the distance to cancel into a late-hit W.623P combo.
Only works when point blank, but deals more damage.
Alternatively cancel MK directly into a special move.
A way to spend resources when too far to link 5HP or MK.
Only worthwhile if 360PP can't reach or if that tiny bit of extra drive damage matters.
Huge damage using little resources.
